currentState: Hierarchy exposed and then discarded. Jandice still measures herself by Kel'Thuzad's regard, but chapter 13 proves he left her outside the real room the instant Jaina mattered more.
powerDynamic: Kel'Thuzad holds the right to dismiss, exclude, and define importance. Jandice is left with wounded status, secondhand knowledge, and the chance to survive by running first.

stateHistory.currentState: Chapter 12 keeps the same hierarchy and makes it sharper. Jandice can open the door, but the instant Kel'Thuzad speaks she becomes subordinate again — admired enough to be useful, not central enough to remain.

physicalDynamics.pattern: He never appears, but his authority moves her body anyway: she pauses, asks, returns, and leads Jaina lower only after permission comes back.
Chapter 13 makes their dynamic visible through absence: Jandice is physically in the upper levels because Kel'Thuzad sent her away from the conversation below.

nameUsage.jandiceCallsKelThuzad: The master remains the title that shapes her grievance as much as her loyalty.
nameUsage.kelThuzadCallsJandice: No new direct address on page in chapter 13; his power is felt in the fact that she was excluded before Sylvanas ever reached her.

history.event: Jandice presents Scholomance in the way only a true believer can, and the chapter makes clear Kel'Thuzad is content to weaponize that belief.
Chapter 12 shows Jandice trying to present Jaina's progress to Kel'Thuzad with visible pride, only to be dismissed as soon as the real conversation begins.
Jandice tells Sylvanas that Kel'Thuzad sent her out because Jaina could keep up and he wanted the real exchange to himself.
Her testimony proves the hierarchy had already narrowed to two minds in the sanctum before the interruption arrived.
Once the fight below claims attention, Jandice saves herself rather than trying to retake her place in Kel'Thuzad's confidence.

trajectory: Delegated control -> proud subordinate at the threshold -> wounded exclusion -> loose surviving remnant of the hierarchy.
